sect1.title: Monero-&monero-version;
sect1.sect2:
Introduction to Monero
The Monero package provides a daemon and wallet tool for accessing the Monero, or XMR, crypto currency. The wallet tool provided by this package is command-line only.
There is a GUI wallet tool made by the Monero project, but it only has support for Qt-5, no longer in BLFS, and Qt-5 in older versions of BLFS no longer builds properly. Thus, you may have to use a third-party GUI wallet tool, which is not recommended, find a way to build Qt-5, or stick with the command-line wallet tool.
&long-build-time;
Monero Dependencies
Required
Boost, CMake, , , and
Recommended
, libunwind, libusb, and Protobuf
Optional
Doxygen, Graphviz, and gtest

sect2.title: Downloading Monero
sect2.para: Create a list of the needed tarballs that will be downloaded:
Now download the package, its submodules, and needed patches if applicable, using Wget:

screen.userinput: mkdir monero && cd monero && grep -v '^#' ../monero-list | wget -i- -c \ -B &monero-dl-root;

sect2.title: Installation of Monero
sect2.para: There should be a dedicated user and group to take control of the monerod daemon after it is started. Issue the following commands as the &root; user:

screen.userinput: groupadd -fg 66 monero && useradd -c "Monero Full Node" -d /var/lib/monero -u 66 \ -g monero -s /bin/false monero
for i in *.tar.?z*; do echo "Extracting $i..." tar -xf $i done

sect2.para: Now that everything has been extracted, the required extracted submodules need to be moved into monero-&monero-version;. Do that by running the following commands:

screen.userinput: mv -T RandomX-* monero-&monero-version;/external/randomx && mv -T rapidjson-* monero-&monero-version;/external/rapidjson && mv -T supercop-* monero-&monero-version;/external/supercop && mv -T trezor-common* monero-&monero-version;/external/trezor-common

sect2.para: Now go into the monero-&monero-version; directory:
Ensure Protobuf has the right C++ standard set:
Install Monero by running the following commands:

screen.userinput:
mkdir build && cd build &&
cmake -D CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr \ -D CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \ -D BUILD_GUI_DEPS=ON \ -G Ninja -W no-dev .. &&
ninja

sect2.para: Now install Systemd-specific files as the &root; user:

screen.userinput:
install -vDm644 ../utils/conf/monerod.conf -t /etc/ && install -vDm644 ../utils/systemd/monerod.service -t /usr/lib/systemd/system/ &&
install -vDm644 /dev/stdin /usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/monero.conf << "EOF" d /var/lib/monero 0770 monero monero - - EOF

sect3.para: If you want the monerod daemon to start automatically when the system is booted, run the following command as the &root; user:

note.para: The monerod daemon can take up RAM and CPU resources. This will depend on the system, so you should look at the resources used and see if you want it started at every boot.

sect2.title: Command Explanations
sect2.para: -D BUILD_GUI_DEPS=ON: This parameter ensures the dependencies for the GUI wallet are built, in case the GUI wallet tool gains support for Qt-6, GTK, or something else.

seglistitem.seg: monero-blockchain-ancestry, monero-blockchain-depth, monero-blockchain-export, monero-blockchain-import, monero-blockchain-mark-spent-outputs, monero-blockchain-prune, monero-blockchain-prune-known-spent-data, monero-blockchain-stats, monero-blockchain-usage, monerod, monero-gen-ssl-cert, monero-gen-trusted-multisig, monero-wallet-cli, and monero-wallet-rpc

listitem.para: allows creating and working with a Monero wallet
is the RPC version of the CLI wallet
